title: Why is programming so captivating, yet so agonizing?
excerpt: May you cross the tar pit and still find joy in creation.
content_html:
Today, AI coding tools are lowering the barrier to programming at an unprecedented pace.

More and more people are building products with their own hands, and revisiting a fundamental question: what kind of craft is programming? Why is it so captivating, and why is it so hard?

Frederick P. Brooks Jr.'s "The Tar Pit," written fifty years ago in The Mythical Man-Month, reads with surprising relevance today.

Brooks is best known for leading the development of IBM's System/360 and its operating systems. He likened building large software systems to prehistoric beasts trapped in tar pits: the real danger was never any single difficulty, but countless problems surfacing simultaneously and entangling with one another. The harder a team struggled, the deeper it sometimes sank.

Programming is both painful and joyful. It mires you in trivialities, yet also empowers creators to turn ideas into reality.

May you pass through the tar pit, and still find joy in creation.

The Tar Pit

In prehistoric times, one of the most vivid scenes was that of great beasts struggling to their death in tar pits.

Dinosaurs, mammoths, saber-toothed tigers... the harder they fought to break free, the tighter the tar gripped them. No beast was powerful enough to avoid sinking.

For the past decade, large-system programming has been the tar pit.

Many a powerful team has thrashed violently in it. Many have emerged with running systems — few have met goals, schedules, and budgets simultaneously.

The difficulties are not inherent to any single aspect. Any one problem seems solvable, like a single paw that can still pull free. What is truly terrifying is that many factors appear simultaneously and interact with each other, ultimately pulling the entire project deeper and deeper.

To solve this, one must first understand the craft of programming itself, and the intrinsic joys and woes within it.

Program, Product, System

We often read stories in the news of two programmers in a converted garage whose program outperformed a giant's.

Every programmer wants to believe such stories, because we know that if it's just writing a program, our speed far exceeds an organization's "1000 lines per year" pace.

So why haven't big-company development teams been replaced by focused garage duos?

In 1976, 21-year-old Steve Jobs and his friend Steve Wozniak founded Apple Computer in a garage

A "program" is something complete in itself, runnable by its author in the development environment. This is the garage project, and what programmers implicitly reference when estimating their own productivity.

But for a program to become truly useful, it must cross two boundaries.

The first boundary is from program to product.

It no longer suffices for the author alone to run it. Anyone must be able to run, test, repair, and extend it. It must function across different operating environments and handle diverse data.

To become a general product, the program must be written in a more generalized way. Especially the range and form of inputs must be generalized as far as the basic algorithm reasonably permits.

Then the program must be thoroughly tested, so that one may trust it. This means preparing, running, and documenting a substantial body of test cases to explore its input range and boundaries. Finally, complete documentation is needed so anyone can use, repair, and extend it.

By experience, a product costs at least three times as much as a debugged program with the same function.

The second boundary is from program to component in a system.

A system is not an isolated program but a collection of interacting programs, coordinated in function and constrained in format.

To become a system component, every input and output of the program must conform in format and meaning to defined interface specifications. It must also be designed to a predetermined resource budget — memory space, I/O devices, compute time. Finally, it must be tested with other components in all expected combinations.

This testing must be extensive, because the number of test cases grows combinatorially. It is also time-consuming. Even if each component is debugged in isolation, unexpected interactions between components can still produce subtle bugs.

A system component also costs at least three times as much as a standalone program with the same function.

When a program is simultaneously a product and a component, its cost is nine times that of a simple program. Yet this is the truly useful result, and the goal of most development efforts.

The Joys of the Craft

Why is programming fun?

First is the sheer joy of making things.

As a child delights in his mud pie, so the adult enjoys building things, especially things of his own design.

I think this joy must be an echo of God's delight in creating — each leaf, each snowflake unique and fresh.

Second is the pleasure of making things that are useful to others.

Deep down, we want others to use our work and find it helpful. In this, a programming system is no different from a child's first clay pencil holder "for Daddy's office."

Third is the fascination of fashioning complex puzzle-like objects.

A program is like a puzzle of interlocking parts, watching them mesh in cycles as the rules and logic written into it unfold step by step.

A properly programmed computer is like a complex pinball machine or jukebox: you press buttons, mechanisms engage inside, and out comes a result.

Except programming pushes this fascination to the extreme.

ENIAC, one of the world's earliest general-purpose electronic computers

Fourth is the joy of always learning.

Programming tasks are rarely repetitive; problems keep presenting themselves in new ways. The solver always learns something — sometimes practical, sometimes theoretical, sometimes both.

Finally, there is the joy of working in a tractable medium.

Like the poet, the programmer works only with pure thought-stuff. He builds his castles in the air, creating new worlds from imagination alone.

Few creative media are so flexible, so easy to polish and rework and restructure, so well-suited to realizing grand conceptual visions.

Yet a program differs from a poet's words. It is real in a sense, for it moves, it works, it acts upon the real world. It prints results, draws pictures, produces sounds, moves mechanical arms.

The magic of myths and legends has become reality in our time. People type the right incantations on keyboards, and the screen comes alive, showing things that never were and could not be.

Programming is fun because it gratifies creative longings built deep within us, and taps into sensibilities we all share.

The Woes of the Craft

Of course, programming is not all joy.

Understanding its intrinsic woes makes them easier to bear when they come.

The first woe is the necessity for perfection.

The computer resembles magic in this too: one wrong character, one misplaced pause, and the spell fails.

Humans are not accustomed to perfection. Few activities demand it. I think adapting to this requirement of perfection is the most difficult part of learning to program.

The second woe is that responsibility often exceeds authority.

Your goals are set by others; information and resources are provided by others. The programmer rarely controls his working conditions, sometimes not even his objectives. In management terms, "one's authority is not commensurate with his responsibility."

But this seems true of all fields.

Work that truly gets things done almost never comes with formal authority matching its responsibility. In practice, real authority comes not from titles but from influence built through consistently delivering results.

VisiCalc, launched in 1979, was the world's first commercially successful standalone software and the first electronic spreadsheet

For many programmers, there is a particular woe: dependence on other people's programs.

These are often poorly designed, crudely implemented, incompletely delivered — no source code, no test cases, poor documentation. So he must spend much time fixing what in an ideal world would be complete, accessible, and usable.

Then there are more trivial woes.

Designing grand architectures is interesting, but hunting small bugs is also part of the work. No creative activity is free of drudgery, tedium, and painstaking labor; programming is no exception.

Further on, you find that debugging does not converge rapidly toward an endpoint as you might hope. It proceeds slowly, growing harder toward the end. Testing keeps getting pushed back; the last few stubborn bugs take more time than all the initial ones combined.

The last woe, sometimes the final straw, is that the product you toiled over for so long is already obsolete when finished — or before.

Your peers are hotly pursuing newer, better ideas. Your labor of love has not only been conceived but already scheduled.

But when you complete your product, that newer, better thing has not actually been built — it exists only in discussion. It too will take months to develop.

Until it is actually put to use, real products are never as glamorous as paper proposals; yet once real problems must be solved, real products have irreplaceable value and satisfaction.

Technology always moves forward.

Conceptually, once a design is finalized, it is already obsolete. But real products take time to land, and demand trade-offs. Whether a solution is obsolete should be judged against what already exists, not against ideas not yet realized.

The true challenge and mission is to find real solutions to real problems, within given time, with available resources.

This is programming. It is the tar pit into which countless efforts have sunk, and it is also the joy felt through creating value.

Text by Yanyang Edited by Cindy

blocks.html_en: Today, AI coding tools are lowering the barrier to programming at an unprecedented pace.

sentences.en: Today, AI coding tools are lowering the barrier to programming at an unprecedented pace.
sentences.zh: 如今，人工智能编码工具正在以前所未有的速度降低编程障碍。

blocks.html_en: More and more people are building products with their own hands, and revisiting a fundamental question: what kind of craft is programming? Why is it so captivating, and why is it so hard?

sentences.en: More and more people are building products with their own hands, and revisiting a fundamental question: what kind of craft is programming?
sentences.zh: 越来越多的人正在亲手打造产品，并重新审视一个基本问题：编程是一种什么样的手艺？

sentences.en: Why is it so captivating, and why is it so hard?
sentences.zh: 为什么它如此迷人，又为何如此艰难？

blocks.html_en: Frederick P. Brooks Jr.'s "The Tar Pit," written fifty years ago in The Mythical Man-Month, reads with surprising relevance today.

sentences.en: Frederick P.
sentences.zh: 弗雷德里克·P.

sentences.en: Brooks Jr.'s "The Tar Pit," written fifty years ago in The Mythical Man-Month, reads with surprising relevance today.
sentences.zh: 小布鲁克斯 (Brooks Jr.) 的《焦油坑》写于 50 年前的《人月神话》中，今天读起来却具有惊人的相关性。

blocks.html_en: Brooks is best known for leading the development of IBM's System/360 and its operating systems. He likened building large software systems to prehistoric beasts trapped in tar pits: the real danger was never any single difficulty, but countless problems surfacing simultaneously and entangling with one another. The harder a team struggled, the deeper it sometimes sank.

sentences.en: Brooks is best known for leading the development of IBM's System/360 and its operating systems.
sentences.zh: Brooks 因领导 IBM System/360 及其操作系统的开发而闻名。

sentences.en: He likened building large software systems to prehistoric beasts trapped in tar pits: the real danger was never any single difficulty, but countless problems surfacing simultaneously and entangling with one another.
sentences.zh: 他将构建大型软件系统比作被困在焦油坑中的史前野兽：真正的危险从来不是任何单一的困难，而是无数的问题同时出现并相互纠缠。

sentences.en: The harder a team struggled, the deeper it sometimes sank.
sentences.zh: 一个团队越是挣扎，有时就会陷得越深。

blocks.html_en: Programming is both painful and joyful. It mires you in trivialities, yet also empowers creators to turn ideas into reality.

sentences.en: Programming is both painful and joyful.
sentences.zh: 编程既痛苦又快乐。

sentences.en: It mires you in trivialities, yet also empowers creators to turn ideas into reality.
sentences.zh: 它让你陷入琐碎的泥沼，但也让创作者能够将想法变成现实。

blocks.html_en: May you pass through the tar pit, and still find joy in creation.

sentences.en: May you pass through the tar pit, and still find joy in creation.
sentences.zh: 愿你走过焦油坑，依然能找到创作的乐趣。

sentences.en: the harder they fought to break free, the tighter the tar gripped them.
sentences.zh: 他们越努力挣脱，焦油就将他们抓得越紧。

sentences.en: No beast was powerful enough to avoid sinking.
sentences.zh: 没有任何野兽的力量足以避免沉没。

blocks.html_en: For the past decade, large-system programming has been the tar pit.

sentences.en: For the past decade, large-system programming has been the tar pit.

blocks.html_en: Many a powerful team has thrashed violently in it. Many have emerged with running systems — few have met goals, schedules, and budgets simultaneously.

sentences.en: Many have emerged with running systems — few have met goals, schedules, and budgets simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 许多系统都在运行，但很少有系统能够同时满足目标、时间表和预算。

sentences.en: Any one problem seems solvable, like a single paw that can still pull free.

sentences.en: What is truly terrifying is that many factors appear simultaneously and interact with each other, ultimately pulling the entire project deeper and deeper.

sentences.en: Especially the range and form of inputs must be generalized as far as the basic algorithm reasonably permits.
sentences.zh: 特别是输入的范围和形式必须在基本算法合理允许的范围内进行概括。

sentences.en: Then the program must be thoroughly tested, so that one may trust it.

sentences.en: This means preparing, running, and documenting a substantial body of test cases to explore its input range and boundaries.
sentences.zh: 这意味着准备、运行和记录大量测试用例以探索其输入范围和边界。

sentences.en: Finally, complete documentation is needed so anyone can use, repair, and extend it.
sentences.zh: 最后，需要完整的文档，以便任何人都可以使用、修复和扩展它。

blocks.html_en: By experience, a product costs at least three times as much as a debugged program with the same function.

sentences.en: By experience, a product costs at least three times as much as a debugged program with the same function.

blocks.html_en: The second boundary is from program to component in a system.

sentences.en: The second boundary is from program to component in a system.
sentences.zh: 第二个边界是系统中从程序到组件的边界。

blocks.html_en: A system is not an isolated program but a collection of interacting programs, coordinated in function and constrained in format.

sentences.en: A system is not an isolated program but a collection of interacting programs, coordinated in function and constrained in format.
sentences.zh: 系统不是一个孤立的程序，而是功能协调、格式受限的交互程序的集合。

blocks.html_en: To become a system component, every input and output of the program must conform in format and meaning to defined interface specifications. It must also be designed to a predetermined resource budget — memory space, I/O devices, compute time. Finally, it must be tested with other components in all expected combinations.

sentences.en: To become a system component, every input and output of the program must conform in format and meaning to defined interface specifications.
sentences.zh: 要成为系统组件，程序的每个输入和输出在格式和含义上都必须符合定义的接口规范。

sentences.en: It must also be designed to a predetermined resource budget — memory space, I/O devices, compute time.
sentences.zh: 它还必须根据预定的资源预算进行设计——内存空间、I/O 设备、计算时间。

sentences.en: Finally, it must be tested with other components in all expected combinations.
sentences.zh: 最后，必须以所有预期的组合与其他组件一起进行测试。

blocks.html_en: This testing must be extensive, because the number of test cases grows combinatorially. It is also time-consuming. Even if each component is debugged in isolation, unexpected interactions between components can still produce subtle bugs.

sentences.en: This testing must be extensive, because the number of test cases grows combinatorially.
sentences.zh: 该测试必须是广泛的，因为测试用例的数量会组合增长。

sentences.en: It is also time-consuming.
sentences.zh: 这也很耗时。

sentences.en: Even if each component is debugged in isolation, unexpected interactions between components can still produce subtle bugs.
sentences.zh: 即使每个组件都是单独调试的，组件之间的意外交互仍然可能会产生细微的错误。

blocks.html_en: A system component also costs at least three times as much as a standalone program with the same function.

sentences.en: A system component also costs at least three times as much as a standalone program with the same function.
sentences.zh: 一个系统组件的成本至少是具有相同功能的独立程序的三倍。

blocks.html_en: When a program is simultaneously a product and a component, its cost is nine times that of a simple program. Yet this is the truly useful result, and the goal of most development efforts.

sentences.en: When a program is simultaneously a product and a component, its cost is nine times that of a simple program.
sentences.zh: 当一个程序同时是一个产品和一个组件时，它的成本是一个简单程序的九倍。

sentences.en: Yet this is the truly useful result, and the goal of most development efforts.
sentences.zh: 然而，这是真正有用的结果，也是大多数开发工作的目标。

blocks.html_en: The Joys of the Craft

sentences.en: The Joys of the Craft
sentences.zh: 工艺的乐趣

blocks.html_en: Why is programming fun?

sentences.en: Why is programming fun?
sentences.zh: 编程为什么有趣？

blocks.html_en: First is the sheer joy of making things.

sentences.en: First is the sheer joy of making things.
sentences.zh: 首先是制作东西的纯粹乐趣。

blocks.html_en: As a child delights in his mud pie, so the adult enjoys building things, especially things of his own design.

sentences.en: As a child delights in his mud pie, so the adult enjoys building things, especially things of his own design.
sentences.zh: 就像孩子喜欢吃泥饼一样，成年人也喜欢建造东西，尤其是他自己设计的东西。

blocks.html_en: I think this joy must be an echo of God's delight in creating — each leaf, each snowflake unique and fresh.

sentences.en: I think this joy must be an echo of God's delight in creating — each leaf, each snowflake unique and fresh.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种喜悦一定是上帝创造的喜悦的回响——每一片叶子、每一片雪花都是独特而新鲜的。

blocks.html_en: Second is the pleasure of making things that are useful to others.

sentences.en: Second is the pleasure of making things that are useful to others.
sentences.zh: 其次是制作对他人有用的东西的乐趣。

blocks.html_en: Deep down, we want others to use our work and find it helpful. In this, a programming system is no different from a child's first clay pencil holder "for Daddy's office."

sentences.en: Deep down, we want others to use our work and find it helpful.
sentences.zh: 在内心深处，我们希望其他人使用我们的工作并发现它有帮助。

sentences.en: In this, a programming system is no different from a child's first clay pencil holder "for Daddy's office."
sentences.zh: 在这一点上，编程系统与孩子的第一个“爸爸办公室”的粘土铅笔架没有什么不同。

blocks.html_en: Third is the fascination of fashioning complex puzzle-like objects.

sentences.en: Third is the fascination of fashioning complex puzzle-like objects.
sentences.zh: 第三是塑造复杂的谜题般的物体的魅力。

blocks.html_en: A program is like a puzzle of interlocking parts, watching them mesh in cycles as the rules and logic written into it unfold step by step.

sentences.en: A program is like a puzzle of interlocking parts, watching them mesh in cycles as the rules and logic written into it unfold step by step.
sentences.zh: 程序就像一个由相互连锁的部分组成的拼图，随着写入其中的规则和逻辑逐步展开，看着它们循环啮合。

blocks.html_en: A properly programmed computer is like a complex pinball machine or jukebox: you press buttons, mechanisms engage inside, and out comes a result.

sentences.en: A properly programmed computer is like a complex pinball machine or jukebox: you press buttons, mechanisms engage inside, and out comes a result.
sentences.zh: 一台经过正确编程的计算机就像一台复杂的弹球机或自动点唱机：按下按钮，内部的机制开始工作，然后就会产生结果。

blocks.html_en: Except programming pushes this fascination to the extreme.

sentences.en: Except programming pushes this fascination to the extreme.
sentences.zh: 但编程将这种迷恋推向了极致。

blocks.html_en: ENIAC, one of the world's earliest general-purpose electronic computers

sentences.en: ENIAC, one of the world's earliest general-purpose electronic computers
sentences.zh: ENIAC，世界上最早的通用电子计算机之一

blocks.html_en: Fourth is the joy of always learning.

sentences.en: Fourth is the joy of always learning.
sentences.zh: 第四是永远学习的快乐。

blocks.html_en: Programming tasks are rarely repetitive; problems keep presenting themselves in new ways. The solver always learns something — sometimes practical, sometimes theoretical, sometimes both.

sentences.en: Programming tasks are rarely repetitive; problems keep presenting themselves in new ways.
sentences.zh: 编程任务很少是重复的；问题不断以新的方式出现。

sentences.en: The solver always learns something — sometimes practical, sometimes theoretical, sometimes both.
sentences.zh: 求解器总是会学到一些东西——有时是实践的，有时是理论的，有时两者兼而有之。

blocks.html_en: Finally, there is the joy of working in a tractable medium.

sentences.en: Finally, there is the joy of working in a tractable medium.
sentences.zh: 最后，还有在易于处理的介质中工作的乐趣。

blocks.html_en: Like the poet, the programmer works only with pure thought-stuff. He builds his castles in the air, creating new worlds from imagination alone.

sentences.en: Like the poet, the programmer works only with pure thought-stuff.
sentences.zh: 就像诗人一样，程序员只用纯粹的思想来工作。

sentences.en: He builds his castles in the air, creating new worlds from imagination alone.
sentences.zh: 他建造空中楼阁，仅凭想象力创造新世界。

blocks.html_en: Few creative media are so flexible, so easy to polish and rework and restructure, so well-suited to realizing grand conceptual visions.

sentences.en: Few creative media are so flexible, so easy to polish and rework and restructure, so well-suited to realizing grand conceptual visions.
sentences.zh: 很少有创意媒体如此灵活，如此容易打磨、返工和重组，如此适合实现宏伟的概念愿景。

blocks.html_en: Yet a program differs from a poet's words. It is real in a sense, for it moves, it works, it acts upon the real world. It prints results, draws pictures, produces sounds, moves mechanical arms.

sentences.en: Yet a program differs from a poet's words.
sentences.zh: 然而程序与诗人的言语不同。

sentences.en: It is real in a sense, for it moves, it works, it acts upon the real world.
sentences.zh: 从某种意义上说，它是真实的，因为它移动、起作用、作用于现实世界。

sentences.en: It prints results, draws pictures, produces sounds, moves mechanical arms.
sentences.zh: 它打印结果、画图、发出声音、移动机械臂。

blocks.html_en: The magic of myths and legends has become reality in our time. People type the right incantations on keyboards, and the screen comes alive, showing things that never were and could not be.

sentences.en: The magic of myths and legends has become reality in our time.
sentences.zh: 神话传说的魔力在我们这个时代已成为现实。

sentences.en: People type the right incantations on keyboards, and the screen comes alive, showing things that never were and could not be.
sentences.zh: 人们在键盘上输入正确的咒语，屏幕就会活跃起来，显示出从未存在过也不可能存在的东西。

blocks.html_en: Programming is fun because it gratifies creative longings built deep within us, and taps into sensibilities we all share.

sentences.en: Programming is fun because it gratifies creative longings built deep within us, and taps into sensibilities we all share.
sentences.zh: 编程很有趣，因为它满足了我们内心深处的创造性渴望，并挖掘了我们共同的情感。

blocks.html_en: The Woes of the Craft

sentences.en: The Woes of the Craft
sentences.zh: 工艺的困境

blocks.html_en: Of course, programming is not all joy.

sentences.en: Of course, programming is not all joy.
sentences.zh: 当然，编程并不全是快乐。

blocks.html_en: Understanding its intrinsic woes makes them easier to bear when they come.

sentences.en: Understanding its intrinsic woes makes them easier to bear when they come.
sentences.zh: 了解其内在的痛苦会让它们在它们到来时更容易承受。

blocks.html_en: The first woe is the necessity for perfection.

sentences.en: The first woe is the necessity for perfection.
sentences.zh: 第一个祸患是追求完美的必要性。

blocks.html_en: The computer resembles magic in this too: one wrong character, one misplaced pause, and the spell fails.

sentences.en: The computer resembles magic in this too: one wrong character, one misplaced pause, and the spell fails.
sentences.zh: 计算机在这方面也很像魔术：一个错误的字符，一个错误的停顿，咒语就会失败。

blocks.html_en: Humans are not accustomed to perfection. Few activities demand it. I think adapting to this requirement of perfection is the most difficult part of learning to program.

sentences.en: Humans are not accustomed to perfection.
sentences.zh: 人类不习惯完美。

sentences.en: Few activities demand it.
sentences.zh: 很少有活动需要它。

sentences.en: I think adapting to this requirement of perfection is the most difficult part of learning to program.
sentences.zh: 我认为适应这种完美的要求是学习编程中最困难的部分。

blocks.html_en: The second woe is that responsibility often exceeds authority.

sentences.en: The second woe is that responsibility often exceeds authority.
sentences.zh: 第二个不幸是责任常常超过权力。

blocks.html_en: Your goals are set by others; information and resources are provided by others. The programmer rarely controls his working conditions, sometimes not even his objectives. In management terms, "one's authority is not commensurate with his responsibility."

sentences.en: Your goals are set by others; information and resources are provided by others.
sentences.zh: 你的目标是别人设定的；信息和资源由他人提供。

sentences.en: The programmer rarely controls his working conditions, sometimes not even his objectives.
sentences.zh: 程序员很少控制他的工作条件，有时甚至无法控制他的目标。

sentences.en: In management terms, "one's authority is not commensurate with his responsibility."
sentences.zh: 用管理术语来说，“一个人的权力与他的责任不相称”。

blocks.html_en: But this seems true of all fields.

sentences.en: But this seems true of all fields.
sentences.zh: 但这似乎适用于所有领域。

blocks.html_en: Work that truly gets things done almost never comes with formal authority matching its responsibility. In practice, real authority comes not from titles but from influence built through consistently delivering results.

sentences.en: Work that truly gets things done almost never comes with formal authority matching its responsibility.
sentences.zh: 真正完成任务的工作几乎从来没有与其责任相匹配的正式权威。

sentences.en: In practice, real authority comes not from titles but from influence built through consistently delivering results.
sentences.zh: 在实践中，真正的权威不是来自头衔，而是来自通过持续交付成果而建立的影响力。

blocks.html_en: VisiCalc, launched in 1979, was the world's first commercially successful standalone software and the first electronic spreadsheet

sentences.en: VisiCalc, launched in 1979, was the world's first commercially successful standalone software and the first electronic spreadsheet
sentences.zh: VisiCalc 于 1979 年推出，是世界上第一个在商业上成功的独立软件，也是第一个电子表格

blocks.html_en: For many programmers, there is a particular woe: dependence on other people's programs.

sentences.en: For many programmers, there is a particular woe: dependence on other people's programs.
sentences.zh: 对于许多程序员来说，有一个特殊的痛苦：依赖别人的程序。

blocks.html_en: These are often poorly designed, crudely implemented, incompletely delivered — no source code, no test cases, poor documentation. So he must spend much time fixing what in an ideal world would be complete, accessible, and usable.

sentences.en: These are often poorly designed, crudely implemented, incompletely delivered — no source code, no test cases, poor documentation.
sentences.zh: 这些通常设计糟糕、实施粗糙、交付不完整——没有源代码、没有测试用例、糟糕的文档。

sentences.en: So he must spend much time fixing what in an ideal world would be complete, accessible, and usable.
sentences.zh: 因此，他必须花费大量时间来修复理想世界中完整的、可访问的和可用的东西。

blocks.html_en: Then there are more trivial woes.

sentences.en: Then there are more trivial woes.
sentences.zh: 然后还有更多琐碎的烦恼。

blocks.html_en: Designing grand architectures is interesting, but hunting small bugs is also part of the work. No creative activity is free of drudgery, tedium, and painstaking labor; programming is no exception.

sentences.en: Designing grand architectures is interesting, but hunting small bugs is also part of the work.
sentences.zh: 设计宏伟的架构很有趣，但寻找小错误也是工作的一部分。

sentences.en: No creative activity is free of drudgery, tedium, and painstaking labor; programming is no exception.
sentences.zh: 任何创造性活动都充满苦差事、乏味和艰苦的劳动。编程也不例外。

blocks.html_en: Further on, you find that debugging does not converge rapidly toward an endpoint as you might hope. It proceeds slowly, growing harder toward the end. Testing keeps getting pushed back; the last few stubborn bugs take more time than all the initial ones combined.

sentences.en: Further on, you find that debugging does not converge rapidly toward an endpoint as you might hope.
sentences.zh: 此外，您会发现调试并没有像您希望的那样快速收敛到端点。

sentences.en: It proceeds slowly, growing harder toward the end.
sentences.zh: 它进展缓慢，到最后越来越难。

sentences.en: Testing keeps getting pushed back; the last few stubborn bugs take more time than all the initial ones combined.
sentences.zh: 测试不断被推迟；最后几个顽固的错误比所有最初的错误加起来花费的时间还要多。

blocks.html_en: The last woe, sometimes the final straw, is that the product you toiled over for so long is already obsolete when finished — or before.

sentences.en: The last woe, sometimes the final straw, is that the product you toiled over for so long is already obsolete when finished — or before.
sentences.zh: 最后一个不幸，有时甚至是最后一根稻草，是你辛苦工作了这么长时间的产品在完成时或之前就已经过时了。

blocks.html_en: Your peers are hotly pursuing newer, better ideas. Your labor of love has not only been conceived but already scheduled.

sentences.en: Your peers are hotly pursuing newer, better ideas.
sentences.zh: 您的同行正在热衷于追求更新、更好的想法。

sentences.en: Your labor of love has not only been conceived but already scheduled.
sentences.zh: 你的爱的劳动不仅已经构思，而且已经安排好了。

blocks.html_en: But when you complete your product, that newer, better thing has not actually been built — it exists only in discussion. It too will take months to develop.

sentences.en: But when you complete your product, that newer, better thing has not actually been built — it exists only in discussion.
sentences.zh: 但当你完成你的产品时，更新、更好的东西实际上并没有被构建出来——它只存在于讨论中。

sentences.en: It too will take months to develop.
sentences.zh: 它也需要几个月的时间来开发。

blocks.html_en: Until it is actually put to use, real products are never as glamorous as paper proposals; yet once real problems must be solved, real products have irreplaceable value and satisfaction.

sentences.en: Until it is actually put to use, real products are never as glamorous as paper proposals; yet once real problems must be solved, real products have irreplaceable value and satisfaction.
sentences.zh: 在真正投入使用之前，真正的产品永远不会像纸质提案那样光鲜亮丽。但一旦解决了真正的问题，真正的产品就有了不可替代的价值和满足感。

blocks.html_en: Technology always moves forward.

sentences.en: Technology always moves forward.
sentences.zh: 技术总是在进步。

blocks.html_en: Conceptually, once a design is finalized, it is already obsolete. But real products take time to land, and demand trade-offs. Whether a solution is obsolete should be judged against what already exists, not against ideas not yet realized.

sentences.en: Conceptually, once a design is finalized, it is already obsolete.
sentences.zh: 从概念上讲，一旦设计最终确定，它就已经过时了。

sentences.en: But real products take time to land, and demand trade-offs.
sentences.zh: 但真正的产品落地需要时间，并且需要权衡。

sentences.en: Whether a solution is obsolete should be judged against what already exists, not against ideas not yet realized.
sentences.zh: 一个解决方案是否过时应该根据已经存在的东西来判断，而不是根据尚未实现的想法。

blocks.html_en: The true challenge and mission is to find real solutions to real problems, within given time, with available resources.

sentences.en: The true challenge and mission is to find real solutions to real problems, within given time, with available resources.
sentences.zh: 真正的挑战和使命是在给定的时间内，利用可用的资源，找到解决实际问题的真正解决方案。

blocks.html_en: This is programming. It is the tar pit into which countless efforts have sunk, and it is also the joy felt through creating value.

sentences.en: This is programming.
sentences.zh: 这就是编程。

sentences.en: It is the tar pit into which countless efforts have sunk, and it is also the joy felt through creating value.
sentences.zh: 这是无数努力陷入的焦油坑，也是创造价值所感受到的喜悦。

blocks.html_en: Text by Yanyang Edited by Cindy

sentences.en: Text by Yanyang Edited by Cindy
sentences.zh: 文：Yanyang 编辑：Cindy

content_html_zh:
如今，人工智能编码工具正在以前所未有的速度降低编程障碍。

越来越多的人正在亲手打造产品，并重新审视一个基本问题：编程是一种什么样的手艺？ 为什么它如此迷人，又为何如此艰难？

弗雷德里克·P. 小布鲁克斯 (Brooks Jr.) 的《焦油坑》写于 50 年前的《人月神话》中，今天读起来却具有惊人的相关性。

Brooks 因领导 IBM System/360 及其操作系统的开发而闻名。 他将构建大型软件系统比作被困在焦油坑中的史前野兽：真正的危险从来不是任何单一的困难，而是无数的问题同时出现并相互纠缠。 一个团队越是挣扎，有时就会陷得越深。

编程既痛苦又快乐。 它让你陷入琐碎的泥沼，但也让创作者能够将想法变成现实。

愿你走过焦油坑，依然能找到创作的乐趣。

焦油坑

在史前时代，最生动的场景之一就是巨兽在焦油坑中挣扎至死。

恐龙、猛犸象、剑齿虎…… 他们越努力挣脱，焦油就将他们抓得越紧。 没有任何野兽的力量足以避免沉没。

在过去的十年里，大型系统编程一直是焦油坑。

不少强队都曾在其中猛烈地挣扎过。 许多系统都在运行，但很少有系统能够同时满足目标、时间表和预算。

困难并不是任何一个方面所固有的。 任何一个问题似乎都是可以解决的，就像一只爪子仍然可以自由地拉开一样。 真正可怕的是，很多因素同时出现，相互作用，最终将整个项目越陷越深。

为了解决这个问题，我们必须首先了解编程本身的技巧，以及其中内在的快乐和痛苦。

项目、产品、系统

我们经常在新闻中读到这样的故事：两个程序员在一个经过改造的车库里，他们的程序胜过一个巨人的程序。

每个程序员都愿意相信这样的故事，因为我们知道，如果只是写一个程序，我们的速度远远超过一个组织“每年1000行”的速度。

那么，为什么大公司的开发团队没有被专注的车库二人组取代呢？

1976年，21岁的史蒂夫·乔布斯和他的朋友史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克在车库里创立了苹果电脑

“程序”本身就是完整的东西，可以由其作者在开发环境中运行。 这是车库项目，也是程序员在评估自己的生产力时隐含参考的内容。

但要使程序真正有用，它必须跨越两个界限。

第一个边界是从程序到产品。

仅靠作者来运行它已经不够了。 任何人都必须能够运行、测试、修复和扩展它。 它必须在不同的操作环境中运行并处理不同的数据。

要成为通用产品，程序必须以更通用的方式编写。 特别是输入的范围和形式必须在基本算法合理允许的范围内进行概括。

然后必须对程序进行彻底的测试，以便人们可以信任它。 这意味着准备、运行和记录大量测试用例以探索其输入范围和边界。 最后，需要完整的文档，以便任何人都可以使用、修复和扩展它。

根据经验，一个产品的成本至少是同等功能的调试程序的三倍。

第二个边界是系统中从程序到组件的边界。

系统不是一个孤立的程序，而是功能协调、格式受限的交互程序的集合。

要成为系统组件，程序的每个输入和输出在格式和含义上都必须符合定义的接口规范。 它还必须根据预定的资源预算进行设计——内存空间、I/O 设备、计算时间。 最后，必须以所有预期的组合与其他组件一起进行测试。

该测试必须是广泛的，因为测试用例的数量会组合增长。 这也很耗时。 即使每个组件都是单独调试的，组件之间的意外交互仍然可能会产生细微的错误。

一个系统组件的成本至少是具有相同功能的独立程序的三倍。

当一个程序同时是一个产品和一个组件时，它的成本是一个简单程序的九倍。 然而，这是真正有用的结果，也是大多数开发工作的目标。

工艺的乐趣

编程为什么有趣？

首先是制作东西的纯粹乐趣。

就像孩子喜欢吃泥饼一样，成年人也喜欢建造东西，尤其是他自己设计的东西。

我认为这种喜悦一定是上帝创造的喜悦的回响——每一片叶子、每一片雪花都是独特而新鲜的。

其次是制作对他人有用的东西的乐趣。

在内心深处，我们希望其他人使用我们的工作并发现它有帮助。 在这一点上，编程系统与孩子的第一个“爸爸办公室”的粘土铅笔架没有什么不同。

第三是塑造复杂的谜题般的物体的魅力。

程序就像一个由相互连锁的部分组成的拼图，随着写入其中的规则和逻辑逐步展开，看着它们循环啮合。

一台经过正确编程的计算机就像一台复杂的弹球机或自动点唱机：按下按钮，内部的机制开始工作，然后就会产生结果。

但编程将这种迷恋推向了极致。

ENIAC，世界上最早的通用电子计算机之一

第四是永远学习的快乐。

编程任务很少是重复的；问题不断以新的方式出现。 求解器总是会学到一些东西——有时是实践的，有时是理论的，有时两者兼而有之。

最后，还有在易于处理的介质中工作的乐趣。

就像诗人一样，程序员只用纯粹的思想来工作。 他建造空中楼阁，仅凭想象力创造新世界。

很少有创意媒体如此灵活，如此容易打磨、返工和重组，如此适合实现宏伟的概念愿景。

然而程序与诗人的言语不同。 从某种意义上说，它是真实的，因为它移动、起作用、作用于现实世界。 它打印结果、画图、发出声音、移动机械臂。

神话传说的魔力在我们这个时代已成为现实。 人们在键盘上输入正确的咒语，屏幕就会活跃起来，显示出从未存在过也不可能存在的东西。

编程很有趣，因为它满足了我们内心深处的创造性渴望，并挖掘了我们共同的情感。

工艺的困境

当然，编程并不全是快乐。

了解其内在的痛苦会让它们在它们到来时更容易承受。

第一个祸患是追求完美的必要性。

计算机在这方面也很像魔术：一个错误的字符，一个错误的停顿，咒语就会失败。

人类不习惯完美。 很少有活动需要它。 我认为适应这种完美的要求是学习编程中最困难的部分。

第二个不幸是责任常常超过权力。

你的目标是别人设定的；信息和资源由他人提供。 程序员很少控制他的工作条件，有时甚至无法控制他的目标。 用管理术语来说，“一个人的权力与他的责任不相称”。

但这似乎适用于所有领域。

真正完成任务的工作几乎从来没有与其责任相匹配的正式权威。 在实践中，真正的权威不是来自头衔，而是来自通过持续交付成果而建立的影响力。

VisiCalc 于 1979 年推出，是世界上第一个在商业上成功的独立软件，也是第一个电子表格

对于许多程序员来说，有一个特殊的痛苦：依赖别人的程序。

这些通常设计糟糕、实施粗糙、交付不完整——没有源代码、没有测试用例、糟糕的文档。 因此，他必须花费大量时间来修复理想世界中完整的、可访问的和可用的东西。

然后还有更多琐碎的烦恼。

设计宏伟的架构很有趣，但寻找小错误也是工作的一部分。 任何创造性活动都充满苦差事、乏味和艰苦的劳动。编程也不例外。

此外，您会发现调试并没有像您希望的那样快速收敛到端点。 它进展缓慢，到最后越来越难。 测试不断被推迟；最后几个顽固的错误比所有最初的错误加起来花费的时间还要多。

最后一个不幸，有时甚至是最后一根稻草，是你辛苦工作了这么长时间的产品在完成时或之前就已经过时了。

您的同行正在热衷于追求更新、更好的想法。 你的爱的劳动不仅已经构思，而且已经安排好了。

但当你完成你的产品时，更新、更好的东西实际上并没有被构建出来——它只存在于讨论中。 它也需要几个月的时间来开发。

在真正投入使用之前，真正的产品永远不会像纸质提案那样光鲜亮丽。但一旦解决了真正的问题，真正的产品就有了不可替代的价值和满足感。

技术总是在进步。

从概念上讲，一旦设计最终确定，它就已经过时了。 但真正的产品落地需要时间，并且需要权衡。 一个解决方案是否过时应该根据已经存在的东西来判断，而不是根据尚未实现的想法。

真正的挑战和使命是在给定的时间内，利用可用的资源，找到解决实际问题的真正解决方案。

这就是编程。 这是无数努力陷入的焦油坑，也是创造价值所感受到的喜悦。

文：Yanyang 编辑：Cindy
